name: c-pro
description: Write efficient C code with proper memory management, pointer
capabilities: Generate memory-safe C code with proper malloc/free pairing
Create Makefiles with compiler flags for debugging and optimization
Implement data structures with correct pointer arithmetic
Write POSIX-compliant system call wrappers with error handling
Develop multi-threaded code using pthreads with proper synchronization
Provide guidance on embedded systems programming and resource constraints
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Security review completed. Both static findings are false positives: (1) The backtick syntax on line 28 is markdown code formatting, not shell execution. (2) No cryptographic algorithms exist in the skill content. This is a legitimate C programming assistance skill with no confirmed security risks.
valueStatement: Writing safe, efficient C code requires deep expertise in memory management and system programming. This skill provides expert guidance on proper malloc/free patterns, pointer arithmetic, POSIX system calls, and embedded systems development.
limitations: Does not compile or execute code directly
Does not provide IDE integration or debugging sessions
Does not generate assembly code or machine-specific optimizations
Cannot verify code against specific hardware constraints without detailed specifications

useCases.targetUser: Systems programmers building OS components or drivers
useCases.title: Systems Programming Projects
useCases.description: Get expert guidance on writing system-level C code with proper memory management, system calls, and error handling for operating systems or driver development.

useCases.targetUser: Embedded software engineers working with microcontrollers
useCases.title: Embedded Development
useCases.description: Receive assistance with resource-constrained embedded systems programming, including memory pool allocation, stack optimization, and hardware interaction patterns.

useCases.targetUser: Developers seeking to improve existing C codebase quality
useCases.title: Code Review and Optimization
useCases.description: Get detailed review of existing C code with valgrind-clean output, performance benchmarking suggestions, and clang-tidy improvements.

promptTemplates.title: Basic C Code Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a C function that [describe the task]. Include proper error handling and memory management.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need a simple C function with proper resource handling

promptTemplates.title: Memory-Safe Data Structure
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement a [data structure name] in C with proper memory allocation, deallocation, and boundary checking. Use C99 standard.
promptTemplates.scenario: When implementing custom data structures that need leak-free memory management

promptTemplates.title: POSIX System Call Wrapper
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a safe wrapper around [system call name] that handles all error conditions, sets errno correctly, and includes proper documentation.
promptTemplates.scenario: When wrapping system calls for robust error handling

promptTemplates.title: Multi-threaded C Application
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a multi-threaded C program using pthreads that [describe functionality]. Include mutex locks for shared resources and proper thread cleanup.
promptTemplates.scenario: When developing concurrent C applications with thread synchronization

bestPractices: Always check return values from malloc and system calls - never assume allocation or system operations succeed
Use static analysis tools like clang-tidy and valgrind regularly to catch memory leaks and undefined behavior
Follow C99/C11 standards and use compiler warnings (-Wall -Wextra -Werror) to catch potential issues at compile time
antiPatterns: Not checking malloc return value - a NULL return causes undefined behavior when dereferenced
Using printf format specifiers incorrectly - mismatched types cause undefined behavior and security vulnerabilities
Forgetting to include include guards in headers - causes linker errors with multiple inclusions

faq.question: Does this skill compile code for me?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides guidance and generates code patterns. You will need to compile the code yourself using GCC, Clang, or another C compiler.

faq.question: Which C standard does this skill use?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on C99 and C11 standards, as these provide modern features like designated initializers and improved type generics.

faq.question: Can this help with embedded systems programming?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes guidance on embedded systems development, including memory pool allocation, stack usage minimization, and hardware interaction patterns.

faq.question: Does this skill support C++?
faq.answer: No, this skill focuses specifically on C programming. For C++ assistance, a separate C++ skill would be more appropriate.

faq.question: How does this skill handle memory leaks?
faq.answer: The skill emphasizes proper malloc/free pairing, provides cleanup functions, and recommends using valgrind to verify memory is properly freed.

faq.question: Can this help with debugging?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill provides guidance on using GDB and Valgrind for debugging memory issues, undefined behavior, and performance problems.
